title: ASTS Earnings Miss: Analysis of Revenue Shortfall and Cash Burn Concerns
text: AST SpaceMobile reported disappointing Q1 2026 earnings results, missing revenue expectations significantly with reported revenue of $14.7 million versus expectations around $36.58 million. The company's total operating expenses for Q1 climbed to $164.1 million, approximately 1.58 times higher than the $63.7 million in the prior-year period, primarily driven by increased engineering services and administrative expenses. Financial analysts expect AST SpaceMobile to consume about $1.6 billion and $800 million in cash in 2026 and 2027, respectively, with positive free cash flow not anticipated until 2028. The company invested approximately $379.3 million in property, equipment, and spectrum during Q1 2026 and carried long-term debt of roughly $2.97 billion. AST guided for first-quarter adjusted operating costs of $70-80 million and first-quarter capital spending of $350-425 million. The analysis highlights ASTS's capital-intensive, pre-commercial phase with significant ongoing losses, heavy capital expenditures, and substantial projected cash burn through 2027-2028.
